title: podman search does not sanitize the --format input
body:
### Issue Description

When declaring an input, `podman search --format bob` does not properly deal with the input for the `--format` flag.  The command will output:
```
$ podman search registry --format bob
bob
bob
bob
bob
bob
...
```

This should produce an error showing the allowed formatting options.  Anything other than the allowed options should result in an error message.

I have not explored any vulnerabilities that may exist.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  Run `podman search whatever --format anything_other_than_allowed_options



### Describe the results you received

Podman returns:

```
anything_other_than_allowed_options
anything_other_than_allowed_options
anything_other_than_allowed_options
anything_other_than_allowed_options
anything_other_than_allowed_options
anything_other_than_allowed_options
anything_other_than_allowed_options
...
```

### Describe the results you expected

Podman returns an error listing the possible format options.

### podman info output

```yaml
Client:

```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
This is expected behavior, templates allow you to specify anything. There is not requirement that there must be one valid format specifier in them, sure it likely doesn't make sense to not have one but it is no problem.

The main reason this works is because you can do stuff like this
```
$ podman ps -a --format "Name: {{.Names}} Command: {{.Command}}"
Name: silly_visvesvaraya Command: true
Name: boring_bhaskara Command: true
```

Now if you actually use an invalid template then you get an error
```
$ podman search --format "{{.Abc}}" alpine
Error: template: search:1:13: executing "search" at <.Abc>: can't evaluate field Abc in type types.ImageSearchReport
```

Perhaps some examples in the help?
It's nice that you can customize the output like that.
From what I can see, there are two "built in" formats, JSON and GO template.  I was trying to figure out the format specifier for the GO format.  It's not in the docs nor the `--help` output.
```
--format json
```
Works fine for JSON.  But this example is not in the `--help` not the docs.

I couldn't see in the help how to select the GO template format.  And it would be nice to see an example like the one you posted above in the --help section `--format "Name: {{.Names}} Command: {{.Command}}"` in the docs here https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-search.1.html
And in the output of the `--help` command flag.

The docs always show the valid format specifiers https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-search.1.html#format-format

And it also lists `--format "{{.Name}}\t{{.Stars}}\t{{.Official}}"` in the examples

Shell completion should also be able to auto complete them
```
$ podman search --format {{.
{{.Automated}}    {{.Index}}        {{.Official}}     {{.Tag}}          
{{.Description}}  {{.Name}}         {{.Stars}}   
```

If you want to add more examples and it them to --help please make a PR with them
